questions.question: What is a file system interface?
options: A) A low-level interface for accessing hardware devices
B) A high-level interface for managing files and data
C) A part of the operating system that interacts with the user
D) A type of file format
questions.explanation: A high-level interface for managing files and data

questions.question: What is a file in a file system interface?
options: A) A data structure used to store and manage files
B) A type of file format
C) A collection of data stored on a storage device
D) A part of the operating system
questions.explanation: A collection of data stored on a storage device

questions.question: What is the primary function of a file system interface?
options: A) To manage user privileges
B) To manage file systems and storage devices
C) To manage network connections
D) To manage memory allocation
questions.explanation: To manage file systems and storage devices

questions.question: What is the difference between a file and a directory in a file system interface?
options: A) A file is a directory and vice versa
B) A file is a collection of data stored on a storage device, while a directory is a container for files
C) A file is a type of file format, while a directory is a part of the operating system
D) A file is a part of the operating system, while a directory is a type of file format
questions.explanation: A file is a collection of data stored on a storage device, while a directory is a container for files

questions.question: What is the purpose of a file name?
options: A) To identify a file's location on the storage device
B) To identify a file's type and format
C) To identify a file's contents and purpose
D) To identify a file's size and storage requirements
questions.explanation: To identify a file's location on the storage device
